text_blocks.text: The Sinclair Broadcast Group is the largest operator
of ABC stations by numerical total, owning or providing
services to 28 ABC affiliates and two additional
subchannel-only affiliates; Sinclair owns the largest ABC
WABM-DT2/WDB-DT2 in the Birmingham market, which
serve as repeaters of WBMA-LD (the largest low-power
"Big Four" affiliate by market size, which itself is also
simulcast on a subchannel of former WBMA satellite
WGWW, owned by Sinclair partner company Howard Stirk
Holdings). The E. W. Scripps Company is the largest
operator of ABC stations in terms of overall market reach,
larger markets such as Cleveland, Phoenix, Detroit and
Denver), and through its ownership of Phoenix affiliate
KNXV, Las Vegas affiliate KTW-TV and Tucson
affiliate KGUN-TV, the only provider of ABC programming
for the majority of Arizona (outside of the Yuma-El Centro
